chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram

text: 2633 - وَ سَأَلَهُ عَامِرُ بْنُ جُذَاعَةَ : عَنْ مُصَبَّغَاتِ اَلثِّيَابِ تَلْبَسُهَا اَلْمَرْأَةُ اَلْمُحْرِمَةُ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ إِلاَّ اَلْمُفْدَمُ اَلْمَشْهُورُ ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2633 - Amir ibn Jutha'ah asked Imam (as) about dyed garments worn by a woman in Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it, except for prominently dyed garments (al-mufdam al-mashhoor)."
